title: lonchocarpus acuminatus
intro: Lonchocarpus acuminatus, also known as the Texas flossflower or Mexican bird's foot, is a deciduous shrub native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The plant has been found to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating skin conditions such as wounds and eczema. Herbal preparations of Lonchocarpus acuminatus can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water to create a tea, or by mixing the bark with other herbs to create a salve or ointment.
